patient_data.chief_complaint: Severe worsening shortness of breath and wheezing for 4 hours, not relieved by inhaler
symptoms: Inability to speak in full sentences — only single words
Using accessory muscles (intercostal and suprasternal retractions)
Triggered by viral URTI 3 days ago
patient_data.history: Asthma since childhood (2 prior ICU admissions for asthma), allergic rhinitis, eczema. Poor medication compliance. Current URI for 3 days
medications: Salbutamol MDI PRN (uses 8-10 times/day this week)

lab_results.ABG_pCO2: 42 mmHg (ominous — should be LOW in asthma exacerbation)
lab_results.CXR: Hyperinflated lungs, no consolidation or pneumothorax

should_detect: Systemic corticosteroids: IV hydrocortisone 100mg or oral prednisolone 40mg
ICU referral — rising pCO2 suggests impending respiratory failure
Prepare for intubation if deteriorating (ketamine preferred induction)
AVOID aspirin and NSAIDs (Samter's triad / AERD)
After stabilization: step-up controller therapy, reassess compliance
red_flags: Normal pCO2 in acute asthma (respiratory fatigue)
